title: Initiative Overview
subtitle: Research Infrastructure and Collaboration Opportunity
summary: A concise overview of Sutton SignWriting as globally used but institutionally fragile research infrastructure.
synopsis: Sutton SignWriting is globally used but institutionally fragile infrastructure. This deck explains why research collaboration, grant alignment, and durable administrative support are needed to move from independent maintenance to long-term sustainability.

slides.title: Sutton SignWriting Infrastructure Initiative
slides.summary: Sutton SignWriting is globally used language infrastructure that needs durable institutional support.
bullets: Standardized writing system for signed languages
Used in education, research, and communication
Supports literacy, accessibility, and linguistic analysis
slides.speech: Sutton SignWriting is a standardized system for writing signed languages, used internationally in education, research, and communication. It enables direct written representation of signed languages, supporting literacy, accessibility, and linguistic analysis.
slides.notes: Open with the system itself before talking about fragility.

slides.title: Current Situation
slides.summary: The infrastructure is important, but its maintenance model is fragile.
slides.speech: SignWriting functions as critical infrastructure for a global user base but depends on individual maintenance and limited resources. A recent outage disrupted educational programs in Brazil, highlighting both its importance and its vulnerability.
slides.notes: Keep the outage example concrete without making the whole talk Brazil-only.

slides.title: Opportunity
slides.summary: The initiative is seeking a PI or academic collaborator to connect the work to research and funding structures.
slides.speech: This initiative seeks a Principal Investigator or academic collaborator to help integrate SignWriting into a research and funding framework. SignWriting can serve as shared research infrastructure, enabling new studies and external funding.
slides.notes: This is the grant and academic invitation slide.

slides.title: Collaboration Focus
slides.summary: There are several clear lanes for collaboration and support.
slides.speech: The collaboration focus includes grant development through NIH, NSF, NEH, and international proposals; University of Iowa administered giving; research integration in literacy, cognition, and AI; and partial staff support tied to existing labs and institutional structures.
slides.notes: Show that the work is practical, not abstract.

slides.title: Platform Development and Institutional Value
slides.summary: Platform V3 and international use make this a strong institutional opportunity.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for platform development and institutional value.
bullets: Structured registration for students, teachers, and researchers
Support for institutional and educational use
Foundation for AI and translation pipelines
slides.speech: SignWriting Platform V3 is intended to provide structured user registration, anonymized demographic data for research, support for institutional and educational use, and a foundation for AI and translation pipelines. That gives institutions access to a globally used linguistic system with strong research and accessibility value.
slides.notes: This is the future-facing systems slide.

slides.title: Call to Collaborate
slides.summary: The initiative is seeking faculty collaborators and institutional alignment.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for call to collaborate.
slides.speech: We are seeking faculty interested in serving as PI or co-investigator, developing grant proposals, helping shape gift and donation pathways through the University of Iowa, and integrating SignWriting into research programs.
